nameMeanings.name: Napier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name from Old French napier, nappier, nap(p)er(er) ‘one who looks after the napery or table-linen’, a derivative of Old French nappe ‘table-cloth, napkin’. In Middle English, Napier and Nap(p)er might also have denoted a maker of table cloths, though this sense has not been recorded.

English: alternatively an occupational name from Nap(p)er from an unrecorded Middle English word for one who makes naps, i.e. drinking cups or chalices (Middle English nap, Old English hnæp). Napper could also be a nickname from Middle English nappere ‘one who sleeps or naps’, though this is the least likely of the alternatives.
American shortened form of Polish   Napierala   or a similar name.

variants.name: Napper
meanings: Altered form of English or German   Knapper   or of German   Nepper  .

variants.name: Nappier
meanings: English: variant of   Napier  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Napierala
meanings: Polish (Napierała): nickname for an insistent person,from a derivative of napierać ‘advance, press, urge’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Mary Napier was born in 1471, in Merchiston, Midlothian, Scotland, her father, John Napier 3rd of Merchiston, was 52 and her mother, Elizabeth Menteith, was 47. She had at least 2 sons and 2 daughters with Sir Henry Preston. She died on 9 September 1513, in Scotland, at the age of 42.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Mary Napier est née en 1471, à Merchiston, Midlothian, Écosse, son père, John Napier 3rd of Merchiston, avait 52 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Menteith, avait 47 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles avec Sir Henry Preston. Elle est décédée le 9 septembre 1513, à Écosse, à l’âge de 42 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Mary Napier 1471 in Merchiston, Midlothian, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Napier 3rd of Merchiston 52 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Menteith 47. Sie hatte mit Sir Henry Preston mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 9. September 1513 im Alter von 42 Jahren in Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Mary Napier è nata il nel 1471 nel Merchiston, Midlothian, Scotland, sua padre, John Napier 3rd of Merchiston, aveva 52 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Menteith, aveva 47 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie con Sir Henry Preston. Morì il 9 settembre 1513, nel Scotland, all'età di 42 anni.
